response:
D


The question asks which inquiry best reveals the makeup (e.g., metallic vs. non-metallic, ionic character) of an unknown sample. Options A and B probe transient/external factors (current temperature or market price) that are unrelated to intrinsic chemical identity. Option C inquires about past history rather than a repeatable, characteristic physical or chemical trait. Option D, by contrast, tests electrical conductivity—an intensive physical property directly governed by composition (free electrons in metals, mobile ions in electrolytes, or their absence in insulators). Measuring it therefore supplies diagnostic evidence that narrows possible identities far more effectively than the other three questions.
